channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tell Alexa to ‘Thank My Driver,’ and they’ll get $5 — at no cost to you
item.description: If you tell Alexa to "Thank my driver," your most recent Amazon delivery driver will get a $5 bonus at no charge to you. Shop these Amazon Echo devices to say thanks now.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Tech consultant found guilty of second-degree murder in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A San Francisco jury on Tuesday found a tech consultant guilty of second-degree murder in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee, which carries a sentence of 16 years to life.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US border facilities for migrant children are improving but still need work, court monitor says
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The U.S. still separates some migrant children from parents while holding them after they cross the border despite broad improvements at detention centers in Texas, according to a court-ordered monitor's final report.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s lawyers allege juror misconduct in latest bid to get his hush money conviction dismissed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers are alleging that his hush money conviction was tainted by juror misconduct, opening a new front in their fight to overturn the verdict and throw out the historic case.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: College hockey player sued by opponent over injury she suffered on the ice
item.description: A Michigan State University hockey player has been sued by a former opponent for injuries she suffered on the ice.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The final rule will become effective 120 days after its publication in the Federal Register, coinciding with the first few months of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Democrats divided over Biden administration response to reported drone sightings
item.description: The Biden administration’s response to mounting public concern over alleged drone sightings in the northeast is dividing Democrats on Capitol Hill, where some lawmakers are defending the executive assurances that there’s no threat to public safety and others are bashing the various agencies for prov
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why. New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy and U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge allows testing of tissue samples from George Floyd as officer who killed him seeks new trial
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A judge has granted permission to lawyers for Derek Chauvin to have samples from George Floyd examined as part of the former Minneapolis police officer's efforts to challenge his conviction on a federal civil rights charge stemming from Floyd's death in 2020. U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi returns to US after Mexico disappearance, had no idea about global media coverage: report
item.description: Hawaii woman Hannah Kobayashi, who was reported missing in November, has returned to the United States after she was seen crossing the U.S. border into Mexico weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A timeline of the investigation of the Gilgo Beach killings
item.description: For years, women had been disappearing on Long Island. Then in 2010, investigators searching for a missing woman began finding 10 sets of human remains in the scrub along a barrier island parkway, not far from the sands of New York's remote Gilgo Beach.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Event promoters, hotels and lodging sites soon will have to disclose extra fees up front
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission says it will soon require hotels, vacation rental platforms and live event promoters to disclose any fees up front when they list prices.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 14 detained in armed Aurora, Colorado home invasion are likely illegal gang members: police
item.description: Police in Aurora, Colorado, detained 14 people for an early morning armed home invasion and kidnapping committed at an apartment complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect facing murder charges in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings was charged on Tuesday in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama woman doing well after experimental pig kidney transplant
item.description: Towana Looney is the fifth American given a gene-edited pig organ — and notably, she isn’t as sick as prior recipients who died within two months of receiving a pig kidney or heart.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Madison, Wisconsin, school shooting suspect identified as 15-year-old girl
item.description: Fifteen-year-old Natalie Rupnow was the shooter who opened fire inside Abundant Life Christian School, killing a teacher and teen student, Madison police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida man berated, physically abused child for nearly 30 minutes over missing couch cushion: deputies
item.description: A Florida man is facing child abuse charges after he allegedly screamed obscenities and physically abused a child over a missing couch cushion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California 'Shoplift with a Cop' blitz operation leads to dozens of arrests
item.description: Ventura County authorities arrested 37 adult and juveniles during their weekend operation "Shoplift With a Cop" in addition to seizing narcotics and $2,000 in stolen goods.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Younger Asian Americans navigate something new to their generation: Taking up space
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The chairs stay occupied at 12 Pell. Client after client, they come through the tiny barbershop on a narrow side street in Manhattan’s Chinatown. They come for the cuts, sure. But really, they’re coming for the cool.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Bud Light no longer the best-selling draft beer at bars, data suggests
item.description: The overtaking of Bud Light as America's most popular draft beer has been "years in the making," according to the founder of Draftline Technologies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Deer poacher imprisoned, receives largest fine in Ohio history
item.description: Christopher Alexander, 28, was ordered to pay the maximum restitution fine of $35,071.73, the largest restitution value for a single white-tailed deer in Ohio’s history.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri's death row had nearly 100 inmates in the 1990s. Now, it has 8
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Missouri 's status as one of the most active death penalty states is about to change for one simple reason: The state is running out of inmates to execute. The lethal injection of Christopher Collings on Dec.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former babysitter sentenced to life plus 40 years for murder, child abuse
item.description: Makallie Elizabeth Durham of Corinth pled guilty to capital murder and four counts of felonious child abuse on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Woman wanted for Thanksgiving stabbings taken into custody
item.description: The sheriff's office says Jones was wanted in connection with a stabbing that left a man and woman in non-critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Anderson, NewsNation

item.title: How to contact the Rhode Island cyberattack call center after data breach
item.description: Rhode Island officials urged residents to take immediate steps to protect themselves on Saturday, warning that the hackers could release personal data.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi case officially closed
item.description: Hannah Kobayashi, whose disappearance led to a statewide search and international headlines, returned to the United States on Sunday, bringing an end to the high-profile missing persons case. On Monday, the Los Angeles Police Department confirmed that the case is now closed.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Man sentenced for helping pregnant Chinese women travel to give birth in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to nearly 3 1/2 years in prison for running an extensive business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who would automatically have American citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Ingrid Lewis-Martin, chief adviser to NYC Mayor Eric Adams, resigns and expects to be indicted
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teacher and student killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: A teacher and a student were killed in a shooting at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin, police said Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Devin Willems, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How old are Saturn's rings? Study suggests they could be 4.5 billion years old just like the planet
item.description: New research suggests that Saturn's rings may be older than they look — possibly as old as the planet. Instead of being a youthful 400 million years old as commonly thought, the icy, shimmering rings could be 4.5 billion years old just like Saturn.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teacher and student killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: A teacher and a student were killed in a shooting at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin, police said Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Devin Willems, KLAS, CBS News
